title: Large Gauge Invariance Quantizes The Chern-Simons Level
summary: Place the theory on S^2 times S^1 with one unit of flux through S^2 and a constant temporal holonomy A_0 = s/beta; because the Chern-Simons action shifts by 2pi under the allowed large gauge transformation, its coefficient must be integral.
assumptions: The microscopic electron field transforms under a compact U(1) gauge symmetry.
The effective action is allowed to be defined only modulo 2pi in the path integral.
regime: 2+1-dimensional effective topological response on a closed spacetime manifold.
scope: Quantization step for the Chern-Simons level in Witten Lecture Two.

source_anchors.notes: Witten's closed-manifold argument for the integrality of the Chern-Simons level.

representation: Closed-manifold large-gauge-invariance argument for integer Chern-Simons level.
motivation: This note exists because the Hall-response theorem cannot start from an arbitrary real Chern-Simons coefficient. The first nontrivial step is to show that compact U(1) gauge invariance forces the response level to be an integer.
prerequisite_story: Know the Chern-Simons functional in 2+1 dimensions, why the path integral only cares about the action modulo 2pi, and what counts as a large gauge transformation for a compact U(1) gauge field.
local_context_story: In Witten Lecture Two, this is the opening quantization step: put the theory on S^2 x S^1, thread one unit of magnetic flux through S^2, and vary the temporal holonomy around the Euclidean time circle.
forward_links_story: Once k is known to be integral, the next step is to differentiate the Chern-Simons action and obtain the quantized Hall current. That integer is then matched to the band and many-body Chern numbers.
canonical_questions: Why does the closed-manifold argument use S^2 x S^1 with one flux unit?
Why is an action shift by 2pi allowed but any other shift forbidden?
Where exactly does the integrality of k enter the Hall-response theorem?
scope_boundaries: This argument assumes a compact U(1) gauge field and a topological effective action defined modulo 2pi in the path integral.
It is a response-theory quantization argument, not yet the microscopic derivation of the same integer from band topology.

math_expressions.notes: Unit Dirac flux through the spatial two-sphere.
Value of the Chern-Simons functional on the background with temporal holonomy s.
Allowed large gauge transformation of the temporal holonomy.

step_justification: The electron wavefunction only requires exp(i phi) to be single-valued, not phi itself.
The path integral is unchanged when the action shifts by an integer multiple of 2pi.
Therefore an effective action term k I_CS is gauge invariant only when k is an integer.
admissible_assumptions: Work with a compact U(1) gauge field on the closed manifold S^2 x S^1.
Treat the large gauge transformation of the temporal holonomy as an allowed input rather than formalizing the full gauge-group homotopy theory inside this lemma.
formalization_blockers: The current KB does not yet expose a reusable Lean-side library for the Chern-Simons functional on closed three-manifolds.
The path-integral modulo-2pi convention still needs a more explicit formal wrapper than plain equality of real actions.
retrieval_hints: Use when a Hall-response explanation must justify why the Chern-Simons level is quantized before differentiating it into a current.
